portal_activation.answer: To activate your investor portal account: (1) Check your inbox for an invitation email from noreply@tribexa.com — check spam if not found. (2) Click 'Activate Account' and set a secure password. (3) Complete your investor profile including legal name and entity type. (4) Upload required KYC documents (government-issued ID or driver's license). (5) Your account will be reviewed within 1–2 business days. If you need help, contact support@industryfintech.com.

kyc_requirements.answer: KYC (Know Your Client) is a regulatory requirement to verify investor identity. Required documents vary by entity type: Individual: government-issued photo ID (passport or driver's license). LLC/Corporation: ID of authorized signatory + articles of organization. Trust: Trust agreement + trustee ID. All documents must be current and clearly legible. Upload directly through your portal under 'Profile > Documents'.

accreditation.answer: Under SEC Regulation D, most private offerings require investors to be 'accredited.' An accredited investor is a person with income over $200K/year ($300K jointly) for the past two years, OR net worth over $1M (excluding primary residence), OR certain professional certifications. Accreditation is verified through a third-party provider or by providing supporting documents. Your current accreditation status is visible in your portal under 'Profile > Accreditation'. IFT cannot confirm or deny accreditation approval — this is a compliance decision.

banking_changes.answer: To update your banking information for distributions: (1) Log into your portal at your fund's portal URL. (2) Navigate to 'Profile > Banking'. (3) Enter new bank details and submit. (4) A micro-deposit verification will be sent within 1–3 business days. (5) Once verified, the new account will be used for future distributions. IMPORTANT: IFT will never ask you to change banking information via email. If you receive such a request, contact us immediately at support@industryfintech.com.

tax_documents_general.answer: Tax documents (1099-DIV or K-1, depending on your fund) are made available annually. 1099-DIV: Available by January 31. K-1: Available by March 15. Documents are uploaded to your portal under 'Documents > Tax'. You will also receive an email notification when they are ready. If you believe a document is missing or incorrect, contact your fund's IR contact directly.

investment_status.answer: To view your current investment status, log into your investor portal and navigate to 'Portfolio'. You will see: total invested amount, current NAV (if applicable), distributions received to date, and any pending transactions. If the information appears incorrect, contact support@industryfintech.com with your investor ID and deal name.

regulation_d.answer: Regulation D (Reg D) allows companies to raise capital through private placements without full SEC registration. Rule 506(b) allows up to 35 non-accredited but sophisticated investors, with no general solicitation. Rule 506(c) requires all investors to be accredited, but allows general solicitation. Regulation A allows smaller public offerings (up to $75M) with lighter disclosure. Regulation CF (Crowdfunding) allows small raises from the general public via online platforms. IFT provides this information for general education only and it does not constitute legal or investment advice.

portal_technical.answer: Common technical issues: Forgot password: Click 'Forgot Password' on the login page and follow the instructions. Two-factor authentication issues: Contact support to reset 2FA. Documents not loading: Try a different browser or clear your cache. Portal access denied: Your account may be pending review — allow 1–2 business days after KYC submission. For all other technical issues, email support@industryfintech.com with a screenshot and description.
